title: pyrus neapolitana
intro: Pyrus neapolitana, also known as the Neapolitan pear or Pyrus communis var. neapolitana, is a deciduous tree or shrub native to southern Europe and the Mediterranean region. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal disorders, fever, and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Pyrus neapolitana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the fruit, bark, and leaves of the plant are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate a range of health complaints.
